The Executioner of the Second is that trick given a body and a blade—a faceless headsman from the rewound instant who does not merely slay you, but reaches back and drags a hollow copy of you into the fight to finish what you started. To face it is to risk being made into a weapon against your own friends.

entries.name: Design: CR 17 Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A brutal executioner tier-4 elite in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold (no Legendary Actions or Resistance). It focuses wounded targets and turns the slain against the party with Rewound Copies—a direct mechanical echo of Vorthrax's rebirth curse. A Forgotten Realms-friendly devil-headsman without ties to any named hierarchy.

trait.name: Under-Black
entries: The Princeling is black so deep it reads as a hole in the world—'under-black,' the survivors call it—with stray starlight bleeding through the cracks in its hide. It can't be {@condition Blinded} and ignores Half and Three-Quarters Cover.

trait.name: Devours Light
entries: Magical light of 5th level or lower within 60 feet of the Princeling is suppressed, and Bright Light there becomes Dim Light. Whenever a creature within 60 feet regains Hit Points, that creature regains half as many (the Princeling drinks the rest).

trait.name: Cloak of the Deep
entries: The Princeling is shrouded in heavy magical Darkness in a 15-foot Emanation that moves with it. It and other {@i paradox}-tagged creatures can see through this Darkness.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Princeling casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +12 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Princeling casts a spell, then makes two Devouring Claw attacks.

action.name: Devouring Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 10 ft. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d8 + 8}) Slashing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage, and the target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by the Necrotic damage taken until it finishes a Long Rest.

action.name: The Night That Eats {@recharge 5}
entries: The Princeling exhales the appetite of the Primordial Dark in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave con} {@dc 20}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Necrotic damage, and the creature is {@condition Blinded} until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. The area is plunged into magical Darkness until the start of the Princeling's next turn.

bonus.name: Step Through Shadow
entries: The Princeling teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see in Dim Light or Darkness.

entries.name: The Half-Eaten Avatar
entries: The Primordial Darkness—the mother-deep of the Elemental Realm of Shadow—reached into Gaia to claim the corrupted region, and the paradox bit the hand. The Princeling is what came back: an avatar of the Dark with half its substance unmade, ravenous now for the very thing it lost. It eats light and stolen healing both, and the deeper the night around it grows, the more of itself it remembers. It is the shadow counterpart to the Sundered Archangel; where they meet, the world simply goes out.

entries.name: Design: CR 20 Boss Elite (No Legendary Actions)
entries: A capstone caster-boss in the Astral Tyrannosaur mold: huge offense, 276 Hit Points, high-arcane shadow magic, but {@b no Legendary Actions or Legendary Resistance}. Devours Light makes healing and illumination unreliable—a real check on a well-equipped party—while it teleports through its own darkness. Best run with adds or upgraded with Legendary Resistance (3/Day) and 3 Legendary Actions for a true solo. Counterpart to the Sundered Archangel.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: The Paradox Kaeru is built to do exactly one thing: shuffle up to you and stab once, for every point of damage a Challenge 20 threat is allowed to deal—all of it loaded into a single blow. It has minion-grade Hit Points (99), only one attack, and a slow Speed, so a party that focuses it can drop the little thing before it ever arrives. Its CR reflects the terror of the knife, not its toughness. The joke is that it looks harmless and plods along so slowly... right up until it doesn't miss.

trait.name: Inexorable
entries: The Kaeru can't be {@condition frightened} and can't be knocked {@condition prone}, its Speed can't be reduced below 10 feet, and it always knows the location of the nearest creature it could stab. It comes forward, and it does not stop.

trait.name: Grudge (Everyone's Grudge)
entries: The Necrotic damage of Chef's Knife is the grudge—the weight of every wound ever dealt to the Kaeru's kind, paid back in a single stroke. It always applies at full; there is nothing to track.

trait.name: Lantern
entries: The Kaeru carries a small lantern that sheds Dim Light in a 10-foot radius. It never sets the lantern down.

action.name: Chef's Knife
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}120 ({@damage 20d10 + 10}) Piercing damage plus 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Necrotic damage (the Grudge). This is the only thing the Kaeru does, and it is more than enough.

entries.name: It Is Already Walking Toward You
entries: A small, hunched thing with reptilian skin, a green lantern in one hand and a kitchen knife in the other, shuffling across the battlefield one patient step at a time. It says nothing. It does not hurry. It does not flinch when you hit it, and it does not stop. Veterans of the corrupted region have a rule about the little lantern-bearers: you kill it before it reaches you, or you do not get a second opinion. Whatever it once was, the paradox left it with a single, perfect purpose and a very long memory.

entries.name: Design: CR 20 Glass-Cannon Minion (One Attack)
entries: A 'minion-quality' boss that is pure offense in one swing: its entire CR 20 damage budget is poured into a single Chef's Knife that always lands for a flat ~186 (120 Piercing + 66 Necrotic Grudge)—no tracking, no save, no Multiattack. If it reaches you, it more or less one-shots you. It has no Multiattack, no Legendary Actions, no Legendary Resistance, minion Hit Points, and a 20-foot Speed—so the counterplay writes itself: focus it down or kite it before it closes, and do NOT let your low-HP characters be the nearest target. Field one as a ticking-clock threat, or several to make the party choose who eats a knife. Slow, frail, and absolutely lethal if ignored.

monster.name: Paradox Succubus

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Paradox Succubus fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Two Natures (Monk and Fiend)
entries: The Paradox Succubus is a master of the Way of the Open Palm who is also a true fiend. Each turn she can fight as a {@b monk} (Multiattack and Flurry of Blows), as a {@b succubus} (Charm, Draining Kiss, Etherealness, Shape-Shift), or blend the two—nothing limits her to one style. Run her however best presses the party that round.

trait.name: Channel Duality
entries: She channels holy ki through one open palm and her fiend nature through the other, so her unarmed strikes carry both Radiant and Necrotic energy (included below). Her Radiant and Necrotic damage ignores Resistance to those types.

trait.name: Diamond Soul
entries: The Paradox Succubus is proficient in all saving throws (already included).

trait.name: Evasion
entries: When she makes a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage, she instead takes none on a success and only half on a failure.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: As a Bonus Action, she can shape-shift into a Small or Medium Humanoid she has seen, or back into her true fiend form. Her statistics are the same in each form, except she loses her flying Speed while in Humanoid form. Anything she is wearing or carrying transforms with her. She reverts to her true form if she dies.

trait.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: While on the same plane of existence as a creature she has Charmed, the Paradox Succubus can communicate with it telepathically over any distance.

trait.name: Paradox Corruption (Curse)
entries: A creature cursed by her Annihilation Palm gains 1 Corruption level: a cumulative {@b −2} penalty to attack rolls, saving throws, and spell save DCs, and it can't regain Hit Points while cursed. At 5 levels it is recopied into a paradox-touched thrall (removed from play). {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} or level-5+ {@spell remove curse|XPHB} removes one level; {@spell wish|XPHB} removes all.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes two Open Palm Strikes. She can replace one of them with Charm or Draining Kiss, or with Annihilation Palm if it is available.

action.name: Open Palm Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 1d12 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Radiant damage and 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Charm
entries: The Paradox Succubus targets one Humanoid she can see within 30 feet. The target makes a {@dc 21} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it is {@condition charmed} for 1 day or until she dies or is more than 1 mile from it. The charmed target obeys her telepathic and verbal commands but won't attack its own allies; it repeats the save if it takes damage or is asked to do something obviously harmful to itself or its allies, ending the effect on a success (and becoming immune to this Charm for 24 hours).

action.name: Draining Kiss
entries: The Paradox Succubus kisses a creature {@condition charmed} by her, or a creature that is {@condition grappled} by her, {@condition incapacitated}, or willing, within 5 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Psychic damage; {@actSaveSuccess} half as much. The target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken (until it finishes a Long Rest), and the Paradox Succubus regains Hit Points equal to that amount. The target dies if this reduces its Hit Point maximum to 0.

action.name: Annihilation Palm {@recharge 6}
entries: The Paradox Succubus brings both palms together on one creature within 5 feet, and holy and fiendish energy meet and annihilate. {@b This attack hits automatically}, and on the turn she uses it she can't also use Flurry of Blows. The target takes 72 ({@damage 16d8}) Force damage—the raw energy where her radiant and necrotic palms meet and annihilate—then makes two saving throws:

items.name: Constitution {@dc 20} (the curse / death).
items.entry: On a failure, roll {@dice d100}: {@b 01–10}, the target dies (Death); {@b 11–35}, the target gains 1 level of Paradox Corruption (Curse; see trait); {@b 36–00}, no additional effect. On a success, the target takes only half the total energy damage and isn't subject to the d100 roll.

items.name: Strength {@dc 20} (the impact).
items.entry: On a failure, the target is hurled 100 feet straight away from her and knocked {@condition prone}; if it strikes a solid surface or another creature before traveling the full distance, it stops there and takes an extra 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Bludgeoning damage (and a creature it hits makes the same Strength save or is also knocked {@condition prone}).

action.name: Etherealness
entries: The Paradox Succubus enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. She remains in the Border Ethereal, where she can see and move but can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.

bonus.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes three Open Palm Strikes. Each creature hit by one of these strikes makes a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw or suffers one effect of her choice (chosen per target): knocked {@condition prone}; pushed up to 15 feet; or unable to take {@b Reactions} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Step of the Wind
entries: The Paradox Succubus takes the {@action Dash} and {@action Disengage} actions, and her jump distance is doubled for the turn.

reaction.name: Evasive Flow
entries: When a creature the Paradox Succubus can see makes an attack roll against her, she moves up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. If this movement takes her out of the attack's reach or range, the attack misses (and is wasted); otherwise, the attacker has Disadvantage on that attack. She can use this reaction only once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Paradox Succubus can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Flowing Step
entries: The Paradox Succubus moves up to her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Palm
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes one Open Palm Strike. She can use Palm no more than once per round.

legendary.name: Beguile (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Paradox Succubus uses Charm.

entries.name: The Open Palm of Heaven and Ruin
entries: She was a succubus—an incubus, a lover, a liar, whatever the dream required—until she walked into a paradox-corrupted monastery and stayed to learn. She mastered the Way of the Open Palm and learned to draw holy ki through her fiend's hands, and the paradox rewarded the contradiction: now one palm shines and the other rots, and when she claps them together the two energies meet and unmake whatever is between them. She still seduces, still drinks hearts dry, still slips between worlds—but mostly she fights, beautifully, like a saint and a devil sharing one body.

entries.name: Design: CR 21 Solo Boss for a Legendary-Equipped Level-17 Party
entries: Built as a hard solo for four 17th-level characters kitted with legendary items (2024 DMG: 4x level 17 budgets are roughly Low 18,000 / Moderate 28,800 / High 46,800 XP; a CR 21 solo is ~33,000, a solid {@b Hard}). She wins by never standing still: AC 21, Evasion, Diamond Soul, an Evasive Flow reaction that can waste a single-attack turn, and 60-foot fly speed make her hard to pin.

{@b She fights in three modes (Two Natures), the GM's choice each round:} {@b Monk}—Multiattack (2 Open Palm Strikes) plus Flurry of Blows (3 more) for up to five 1d12+Radiant+Necrotic hits with Open Hand riders (prone / push / no reactions); {@b Succubus}—Charm a frontliner, Draining Kiss to heal off it, Shape-Shift and Etherealness to escape and reposition; or {@b mixed}—Charm someone, then beat down the rest. Lean monk against a martial party, succubus against a squishy/caster party.

{@b Annihilation Palm (recharge 6)} is her finisher and mirrors Granveyra's World-Ending Slash: an auto-hit that deals 72 Force 'annihilation' energy (her radiant and necrotic palms meeting), then a Constitution save against the {@b same} d100 (Death / Paradox Corruption curse) and a Strength save to avoid being launched 100 feet and knocked prone. Telegraph it (she draws her palms apart, light and shadow screaming) so the party can spend a Legendary-Resistance-style item, ready a counter, or simply not be the one standing next to her.

{@b Built-in anti-TPK guards} (so she lands on Hard, not a kill-pace): the legendary Palm fires at most once per round (ceiling of 6 strikes, not 8); she can't use Flurry of Blows on the turn she uses Annihilation Palm (so the finisher can't be stacked with the full flurry onto one PC); and Charm can't turn a PC against its own allies. {@b DM dials.} Too hard: drop her to 2 Legendary Resistances, cut Annihilation Palm's d100 Death band (treat 01–10 as Corruption instead—kept as the Granveyra-parity option the player asked for), or remove the legendary Beguile. Too easy: give her a second Flurry via a 4th Legendary Action use, or let Draining Kiss heal her past her maximum as temporary Hit Points. Against three-plus dedicated healers, remember Paradox Corruption stops the cursed PC from being healed at all.

trait.name: Glass-Cannon Minion
entries: This creature's attacks are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), but its Hit Points are deliberately minion-grade—it hits like an endgame threat and dies like a mook. Its listed CR reflects the danger of its attacks, not its durability: a single solid hit or area spell from a well-equipped 17th-level character usually drops it. For encounter budgeting, treat it as far less durable than a true CR 17 monster (see the Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer for roster math).

trait.name: Paradox-Touched
entries: The goblin was recopied by the paradox around Vorthrax: its hide is tinted violet and its eyes shed faint starlight (Dim Light in a 5-foot radius). It has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}—it has already seen the end of the world.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin takes the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a Bonus Action on each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin makes two Paradox Scimitar attacks.

action.name: Paradox Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage.

reaction.name: Unmade Flicker
entries: When the goblin is reduced to 0 Hit Points, its body tears into a thumbnail of night sky and is gone. Each creature within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 3d4}) Necrotic damage from the imploding seam.

A frontline cutter of the Recopied Warband—a violet shadow of a goblin that swings a blade edged in unmade dark. Part of the Recopied Warband; see the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|VorthraxParadox} for the full encounter.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The goblin casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +12 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The goblin casts a spell or makes two Hex Bolt attacks.

action.name: Hex Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 12}, range 120 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 5d8 + 4}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of the goblin's next turn.

reaction.name: Curse of the Unmade
entries: When a creature the goblin can see within 60 feet reduces it to 0 Hit Points, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or take 22 ({@damage 5d8}) Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn, as a stranger's memories flood in.

entries.name: The Recopied Warband
entries: This warband of goblins and kobolds wandered too long inside the violet ring around Vorthrax's sleeping-hollow, and the paradox recopied them. Each is the same creature it always was—just repainted for the end of the world: hide gone violet, eyes lit with stray starlight, every blow edged with a sliver of the unmade dark. They are louder, faster, and far deadlier than any goblin or kobold has a right to be... and just as fragile as they ever were. The hexer leads, because it remembers leading—over and over, in lives that no longer happened.

entries.name: Design: Glass-Cannon Minions (CR 17)
entries: Every member of the warband is built as a {@b glass cannon}: its attacks, save DCs, and damage are calibrated to Challenge 17 (tier 4), so a single one is a genuine threat to a well-equipped 17th-level character—but its Hit Points are minion-grade (45–66), so it drops to almost any focused hit or area spell. The CR 17 label is the threat of the attack, not the toughness of the body. They have just enough Hit Points to survive the alpha strike of a tier-4 party and take one real turn—which is exactly when they bite.

This breaks the normal CR-to-XP math on purpose. By the book, six CR 17 monsters would be an absurd 108,000 XP; in practice these die so fast that the effective difficulty is roughly that of treating each as a {@b CR 6–7} creature. Budget by their fragility, not their label.

The six variants: {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Cutter|VorthraxParadox} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Clawfoot|VorthraxParadox} (melee warriors), {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Stinger|VorthraxParadox} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Slinger|VorthraxParadox} (archers), and the {@creature Paradox-Touched Goblin Hexer|VorthraxParadox} and {@creature Paradox-Touched Kobold Star-Scale|VorthraxParadox} (casters).

entries.name: Running It for Four Well-Equipped 17th-Level Characters
entries: This is the same party that fights Vorthrax: four 17th-level characters, each with a legendary item and a very rare item. The XP budgets for them are Low 18,000 / Moderate 28,800 / High 46,800. Because these minions are glass cannons, the fight is {@b swingy}—whoever lands the first big turn shapes it. Suggested rosters:

items: {@b Sharp brawl (recommended):} 1 caster + 1 archer + 2 warriors (4 total)—a tense, fast 2–3 round fight that ends when the party's area damage lands.

{@b The full warband:} both casters + 2 archers + 4 warriors (8 total)—genuinely dangerous in round 1 against a tier-4 party, then collapses quickly. Open with the casters' control and the archers' Volley/Scatter before the party scatters them.

{@b Endless tide:} feed them in waves of 4–6 each round to evoke the region recopying its dead—thematically perfect, and it lets you dial lethality live. This is the recommended way to threaten a party this strong: attrition, not a single alpha.

entries: Tactics: casters open with {@spell hunger of hadar|XPHB}, {@spell synaptic static|XPHB}, or {@spell hold monster|XPHB} to pin the party; archers spend {@b Starfall Volley}/{@b Scatter Shot} on clusters; warriors use Nimble Escape and Pack Tactics to gang a single isolated character. Lean into the {@b Unmade Flicker} death bursts—every minion that dies stings whoever killed it, so melee characters take a slow toll wading through the horde.

Flavor note: these are the literal punchline of the campaign's running joke—the level-20 goblin in a different color. When the party recognizes the silhouette of a common goblin and then it hits like a CR 17, that dissonance is the point.

monster.name: False Purple Robes Wizard

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the False Purple Robes Wizard fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Manufactured Image
entries: The wizard is never seen as it truly is. It begins each combat with three illusory duplicates of itself (as the {@spell mirror image|XPHB} spell), and it can remake them with a Bonus Action if it has none.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wizard has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wizard casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +13 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wizard casts a spell, or it makes two Glamoured Bolt attacks.

action.name: Glamoured Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 13}, range 120 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 6d8}) Psychic damage, and the target can't tell the wizard's true location from its duplicates until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Viral Spotlight {@recharge 5}
entries: The wizard turns the eyes of the world on a creature it can see within 120 feet. {@actSave cha} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Psychic damage and the creature has the {@condition charmed} condition for 1 minute, compelled to defend and praise the wizard (it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. Each creature that can see a creature charmed this way must succeed on a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn—the hype spreads.

bonus.name: Spin
entries: The wizard teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see and remakes its Manufactured Image duplicates.

reaction.name: Cancel
entries: When a creature the wizard can see within 60 feet hits it with an attack, the wizard expends an illusory duplicate; the attack instead strikes the duplicate and the wizard takes no damage from it.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the wizard can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Bolt
entries: The wizard makes one Glamoured Bolt attack.

legendary.name: Suggestion
entries: The wizard targets one creature it can see within 60 feet. That creature makes a {@dc 21} Wisdom saving throw or moves up to its Speed in a direction of the wizard's choice.

legendary.name: Encore (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wizard uses Viral Spotlight if it is available.

entries.name: The Media Master, Inverted
entries: The purple robes are the voice of the realm—the ones who spread the word, make the wonders known, and bring everyone to the table. Their false reflection does the opposite with the same tools: it manufactures hype out of lies, charms the crowd into adoring it, and 'cancels' anyone who looks past the glamour. You never fight the real one; you fight the version it wants you to see.

entries.name: Design: CR 21 Caster Boss (Fey, Paradox)
entries: A one-phase legendary control caster built around illusion and charm—standard D&D enchantment/illusion with a light nod to a media/marketing role. Manufactured Image and Cancel make it slippery; Viral Spotlight turns an ally against the party and spreads fear. An Erased mirror of a server's promotion team.

monster.name: False Green Robes Wizard

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the False Green Robes Wizard fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wizard has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Binding Clauses
entries: The wizard's joyless house rules hang in the air around it. Each enemy that starts its turn within 30 feet of the wizard must obey one rule of the wizard's choice until the start of its next turn (for example, 'may not move closer,' 'may not cast spells of level 5 or higher,' or 'may not take the Disengage action'). A creature that breaks its rule takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Psychic damage and the wizard learns it did.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wizard casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +13 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wizard casts a spell, or it uses Errata and makes one Gavel of Judgment attack.

action.name: Gavel of Judgment
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Force damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Errata
entries: The wizard rewrites a creature it can see within 60 feet by fiat. {@actSave int} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} until the end of the wizard's next turn, the target is subject to a 'nerf' of the wizard's choice: Disadvantage on all attack rolls, OR its Speed is halved and it can't take Reactions, OR one class of action (Action, Bonus Action, or Reaction) is banned to it entirely. {@actSaveSuccess} The effect lasts only until the end of the target's next turn.

action.name: Banhammer {@recharge 6}
entries: The wizard slams down a verdict in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} it can see within 90 feet. {@actSave wis} {@dc 21}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage and the creature is {@condition banished} (as {@spell banishment|XPHB}) until the end of the wizard's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and not banished.

reaction.name: Rule Zero
entries: When a creature the wizard can see within 60 feet succeeds on a saving throw or hits with an attack roll, the wizard can force it to reroll and use the new result. Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the wizard can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Gavel
entries: The wizard makes one Gavel of Judgment attack.

legendary.name: Errata
entries: The wizard uses Errata.

legendary.name: Summary Judgment (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wizard uses Banhammer if available, or casts a spell of level 5 or lower from its Spellcasting.

entries.name: The Rules-Keeper, Inverted
entries: The green robes hold the rules so the game stays fair and fun for everyone. Their false reflection keeps the rules too—every petty, joyless, weaponized one of them. It is the lawgiver who reads the fine print to take your turn away, the referee who only ever calls fouls on you. Devils have always understood that a rule is a leash; this one simply forgot that rules were ever meant to be kind.

entries.name: Design: CR 22 Caster Boss (Fiend, Paradox)
entries: A one-phase legendary controller—standard D&D abjuration/enchantment lockdown with a light nod to a rules-and-balance role. Binding Clauses and Errata strip the party's options, Rule Zero cancels their best rolls, and Banhammer removes a cluster from the fight. The Erased mirror of the people who keep a game fair.

entries: When the wizard casts a spell that deals Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder damage, that damage ignores Resistance to its type, and the wizard can choose not to harm a number of creatures of its choice equal to its Intelligence modifier (6).

trait.name: Aura of Avarice
entries: The wizard hoards every spark of power. The first time each turn an enemy within 30 feet casts a spell or expends a charge of a magic item, it takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage and the wizard gains that many Temporary Hit Points.
