entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 13}, range 150 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 5d10 + 6}) Fire or Lightning damage (the wizard's choice).

action.name: Arcane Tithe {@recharge 5}
entries: The wizard collects what it is owed from one creature it can see within 90 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} the target takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Force damage and loses one spell slot (the highest it has) or one charge of a magic item it carries; the wizard regains a spell slot of that level or 20 Hit Points. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no loss.

action.name: Foreclosure
entries: The wizard forecloses on a creature it can see within 60 feet, casting it out of its holdings. {@actSave cha} {@dc 21} or the target is {@condition banished} to a harmless demiplane (as {@spell banishment|XPHB}) for 1 minute (the target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns).

reaction.name: Counterspell
entries: The wizard casts {@spell counterspell|XPHB} (level 5 version) in response to a creature within 60 feet casting a spell.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the wizard can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Bolt
entries: The wizard makes one Evoker's Bolt attack.

legendary.name: Levitate
entries: The wizard flies up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Collect (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wizard uses Arcane Tithe if available.

entries.name: The Patron, Inverted
entries: The red robes keep the lights on—they fund the realm, pay its debts, and make sure the doors stay open for all who come. Their false reflection wears the same crimson, but where the true patron gives, this one only takes: it taxes every spell you cast, forecloses on those who can't pay, and hoards arcane power the way a dragon hoards gold. A tyrant-administrator who confused owning the realm with being owed by it.

entries.name: Design: CR 23 Caster Boss (Aberration/Humanoid, Paradox)
entries: A one-phase legendary blaster in the Red-Wizard-of-Thay mold—standard D&D evocation, plus a resource-drain theme (Aura of Avarice, Arcane Tithe) as a light nod to an owner/funding role. Punishing against caster-heavy parties. The Erased mirror of the people who keep a server paid for and running.

monster.name: False Brown Robes Wizard

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the False Brown Robes Wizard fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wizard has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Patch Notes
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the wizard rewrites one flaw in itself: it ends one condition affecting it and regains 20 Hit Points. Whatever you do to it, it simply edits the rune and tries again.

trait.name: Runebound Automata
entries: The wizard can maintain control of up to four construct minions it has Deployed at once (see Deploy Automaton). While within 60 feet of the wizard, those constructs add the wizard's Intelligence modifier (+6) to their damage rolls—the dread of every optimized fleet that works perfectly until it doesn't.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wizard casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +13 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wizard makes two Rune Strike attacks, or it casts a spell and makes one Rune Strike attack.

action.name: Rune Strike
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d10 + 7}) Force damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Deploy Automaton
entries: The wizard inscribes a command-rune and a guardian construct unfolds in an unoccupied space within 30 feet. Use the statistics of a {@creature Helmed Horror|MM} (or any Large construct of CR 4 or lower), with the {@i paradox} tag. It acts on the wizard's initiative and obeys it. The wizard can have up to four such constructs at once (see Runebound Automata).

action.name: Cascade Failure {@recharge 5}
entries: The wizard introduces a catastrophic bug into the runes of reality in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} it can see within 120 feet. {@actSave dex} {@dc 21}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Lightning and Force damage (half each), and at the start of its next turn the creature must roll a {@dice d6}; on a 1–2 its action that turn simply fails (it 'crashes'). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no crash.

bonus.name: Hotfix
entries: The wizard repairs itself or one construct it can see within 60 feet, restoring 40 Hit Points and ending one condition on the target.

reaction.name: Rollback
entries: When the wizard takes damage, it reverts to a previous state, reducing that damage by 40. Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the wizard can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Strike
entries: The wizard makes one Rune Strike attack, or one Deployed construct under its control makes one attack.

legendary.name: Hotfix
entries: The wizard uses Hotfix.

legendary.name: Deploy (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wizard uses Deploy Automaton.

entries.name: The Devmaster, Inverted
entries: The brown robes are the ones who keep the gears turning—the quiet engineers who inscribe the command-runes, tune the tireless mission-golems, and patch a fault before anyone notices it. Their false reflection is what happens when the runework rots: a construct that no longer optimizes anything, only deploys broken automata, 'fixes' the world into shapes it was never meant to take, and rolls back any blow that lands. It works perfectly. That is the problem.

entries.name: Design: CR 24 Caster Boss (Construct, Paradox)
entries: A one-phase legendary summoner-controller—standard D&D transmutation/conjuration with a medieval-arcane nod to a developer role (no actual code, only runes and automata). Patch Notes + Rollback make it grindy; Deploy Automaton + Runebound Automata flood the field with empowered minions; Cascade Failure can make a hero's turn simply fail. The Erased mirror of the people who keep the machine running—affectionately, the author's own kind.

monster.name: False Blue Robes Wizard

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the False Blue Robes Wizard fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wizard has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Plot Armor
entries: The wizard decides what is true, and the truth is that it survives. The first time the wizard would drop to 0 Hit Points during an encounter, it instead drops to 1 Hit Point and ends all conditions and effects on itself ('that didn't happen'). In addition, a Critical Hit against the wizard is treated as a normal hit.

trait.name: Unreliable Narrator
entries: The wizard's place in the story is never quite fixed. Attack rolls against it that don't come from a creature with Truesight are made with Disadvantage, and it can't be tracked or located by divination magic it doesn't permit.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The wizard casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 23}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +15 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wizard casts a spell, or it makes two Editing Bolt attacks and uses Not Canon.

action.name: Editing Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 15}, range 120 ft. {@h}31 ({@damage 5d8 + 9}) Psychic damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Force damage.

action.name: Not Canon
entries: The wizard declares one creature it can see within 120 feet to be a continuity error. {@actSave cha} {@dc 23}. {@actSaveFail} the target is written out of the scene: it has the {@condition incapacitated} condition, is unable to be targeted or to take any action, and is removed from the battlefield until the end of the wizard's next turn, when it returns to its space (or the nearest space) remembering nothing of the lost time. {@actSaveSuccess} The target instead takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Rewrite the Scene {@recharge 5}
entries: The wizard revises reality in a 60-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on itself. Each other creature there makes a {@dc 23} Wisdom saving throw. {@actSaveFail} 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Psychic damage and the wizard teleports the creature to any unoccupied space it chooses within the sphere (positions are rewritten). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and the creature chooses its new space.

reaction.name: Retcon
entries: When a creature the wizard can see within 60 feet hits with an attack, casts a spell, or deals damage, the wizard declares it never happened: that action is negated and has no effect (a spell is wasted as if {@spell counterspell|XPHB} succeeded; an attack misses; damage is prevented). Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the wizard can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Edit
entries: The wizard makes one Editing Bolt attack.

legendary.name: Not Canon
entries: The wizard uses Not Canon.

legendary.name: Plot Twist (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wizard uses Rewrite the Scene if available, or casts a spell of level 6 or lower from its Spellcasting.

entries.name: The Loremaster, Inverted
entries: The blue robes are the keepers of the story—they decide what is canon, weave the lore into something whole, and make sure the world remembers itself truly. Their false reflection holds the same authority and uses it to lie: it retcons your victories into things that never were, declares your heroes 'not canon' and writes them out of the page, and edits the battlefield itself between sentences. It is the editor who deletes you from the manuscript and dares you to prove you were ever in it.

entries.name: Design: CR 25 Caster Boss (Aberration, Paradox)
entries: A one-phase legendary control/skirmish caster and the strongest of the False Wizards—standard D&D divination/illusion plus reality-editing (Retcon, Not Canon, Rewrite the Scene, Plot Armor) as a nod to a lore/canon role, and the most on-theme for the paradox of Gaia. Retcon negates the party's best turn; Plot Armor gives it a second life; Not Canon temporarily removes a hero. The Erased mirror of the people who decide what is real.

monster.name: Vaelroot, the Verdant Dawn

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Vaelroot fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Verdant Paradox
entries: The ground within 20 feet of Vaelroot churns with too-eager, violet-tinged growth: it is {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} for Vaelroot's enemies. Vaelroot always has terrain to work with—if no natural feature is nearby, it can pull one (a stone, a tree, a patch of grass) up out of the under-sky with Reshape the Scene.

trait.name: Radiant Bloom
entries: Vaelroot sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for another 20 feet. Its Radiant damage ignores Resistance to Radiant damage.

trait.name: Servant of the Paradox
entries: Vorthrax made Vaelroot to tend the corrupted region, and the paradox will not let its gardener die easily. The first time Vaelroot is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it doesn't die: the light drains out of it and the grove darkens as it rises in its shadow aspect, {@creature Vaelroot, the Withering Dusk|VorthraxParadox}, in its space at that form's full Hit Points, with all conditions and effects on it ended. Run all three forms as one boss; award the encounter's XP only when the final form falls.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Vaelroot casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +10 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Vaelroot makes two Bramble Lash attacks, or it casts a spell and makes one Bramble Lash attack.

action.name: Bramble Lash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 15 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Radiant damage, and the target is pulled up to 10 feet toward Vaelroot.

action.name: Reshape the Scene
entries: Vaelroot bends a feature of the battlefield to the paradox. Choose one within 60 feet; the feature is left violet and humming, faintly wrong:

items.name: A stone or boulder.
items.entry: Vaelroot hurls it—{@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 60 ft., {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Bludgeoning plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Radiant damage—or raises it into a 15-foot wall of stone (each 5-ft section AC 17, 30 HP).

items.name: A tree or large plant.
items.entry: It blooms violently: each creature within 10 feet of the plant makes a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Bludgeoning and Radiant damage (half each) on a failure, or half as much on a success. The plant becomes a thicket ({@quickref Difficult Terrain||3}).

items.name: Grass, undergrowth, or soil.
items.entry: Vines erupt in a 15-foot square: each creature there makes a {@dc 18} Strength saving throw or is {@condition restrained} (escape {@dc 18}). The grass then bleaches blank white—a creature that ends its turn in that square takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Psychic damage and can't take Reactions until the end of its next turn.

items.name: No feature present?
items.entry: Vaelroot pulls one out of the under-sky—a stone, tree, or patch of grass erupts in an unoccupied space within 60 feet—then immediately uses one option above.

action.name: Bloomburst {@recharge 5}
entries: Radiant flowers erupt across a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} within 90 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 18}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Radiant damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. The area becomes thorned {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} until the end of Vaelroot's next turn.

bonus.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot melds into a plant or stone within 5 feet and steps out of another plant or stone it can see within 60 feet, through Gaia's scorched veins, leaving a seam of under-sky where it appears.

reaction.name: Thorned Retort
entries: When a creature within 15 feet hits Vaelroot with a melee attack, that creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Piercing damage from lashing brambles.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Vaelroot can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Lash
entries: Vaelroot makes one Bramble Lash attack.

legendary.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot uses Sapway Step.

legendary.name: Reshape (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Vaelroot uses Reshape the Scene.

entries.name: The Gardener of the Wound
entries: Vaelroot was a druid of Gaia before the violet ring took the grove—and then Vorthrax remade it as the corrupted region's gardener. It still loves the land; it simply loves it wrong now, tending rot like a flowerbed and blooming the under-sky where soil used to be. In its first aspect, the Verdant Dawn, it wears the face of spring: radiant overgrowth, blossoms that hurt, a forest in too-bright flower. Kill that, and the season turns.

monster.name: Vaelroot, the Withering Dusk

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Vaelroot fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh.)

trait.name: Verdant Paradox
entries: The ground within 20 feet of Vaelroot is choked with dead, grasping growth—{@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} for its enemies. If no natural feature is nearby, Vaelroot pulls one out of the under-sky with Reshape the Scene.

trait.name: Withering Aura
entries: Bright Light within 30 feet of Vaelroot becomes Dim Light, and a creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of Vaelroot can't regain Hit Points until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Servant of the Paradox
entries: When this form is reduced to 0 Hit Points, Vaelroot doesn't die: the dusk tears open and raw paradox pours out as it rises as {@creature Vaelroot, the Unmade Bloom|VorthraxParadox}, in its space at that form's full Hit Points, with all conditions and effects on it ended. This is its final transformation.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Vaelroot casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +11 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Vaelroot makes two Withering Claw attacks, or it casts a spell and makes one Withering Claw attack.

action.name: Withering Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 15 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain Hit Points until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Reshape the Scene
entries: Vaelroot rots a feature of the battlefield. Choose one within 60 feet; the feature is left blackened and weeping dark sap:

items.name: A stone or boulder.
items.entry: It crumbles into a grasping cairn that lurches at a creature—{@atkr m,r} {@hit 10}, reach 10 ft. or range 60 ft., {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Bludgeoning plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage—or rises as a 15-foot wall of dead stone (each 5-ft section AC 17, 30 HP).

items.name: A tree or large plant.
items.entry: It withers and lashes: each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Necrotic damage on a failure, or half on a success, and the tree's dead branches make the area {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3}.

items.name: Grass, undergrowth, or soil.
items.entry: Dead roots erupt in a 15-foot square: each creature there makes a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw or is {@condition restrained} (escape {@dc 19}). The grass bleaches to blank white—a creature that ends its turn there takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Psychic damage and forgets it has any allies it can't currently see until the start of its next turn.

items.name: No feature present?
items.entry: Vaelroot pulls one out of the under-sky in an unoccupied space within 60 feet, then immediately uses one option above.

action.name: Withering Gale {@recharge 5}
entries: A cold, rotting wind howls in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave con} {@dc 19}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Necrotic damage, and the creature is {@condition frightened} of Vaelroot for 1 minute (it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and not Frightened.

bonus.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot melds into a plant or stone within 5 feet and steps out of another plant or stone it can see within 60 feet, leaving a seam of under-sky where it appears.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Vaelroot can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Claw
entries: Vaelroot makes one Withering Claw attack.

legendary.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot uses Sapway Step.

legendary.name: Reshape (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Vaelroot uses Reshape the Scene.

entries.name: The Season Turns
entries: Cut down the spring and the grove answers with winter. The Withering Dusk is Vaelroot grieving its first death the only way the paradox left it able to: by killing everything green. It drinks the light, rots the trees it once tended, and turns the grass to blank white where the dead forget the living. The land it loved is now the weapon it loves you with.

monster.name: Vaelroot, the Unmade Bloom

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Vaelroot fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh.)

trait.name: Forbidden Color
entries: Vaelroot's Force damage is the raw paradox of mixed light and shadow: it ignores Resistance and Immunity to Radiant, Necrotic, and Force damage. Its Radiant and Necrotic damage ignore Resistance to those types.

trait.name: Unmade Ground
entries: Vaelroot is barely anchored to one place now. The ground within 20 feet of it is {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} of buckling, half-real earth, and it can pull terrain out of the under-sky with Reshape the Scene from anywhere it can see. It can't be {@condition surprised}.

trait.name: The End of the Grove
entries: This is the final form. When the Unmade Bloom is reduced to 0 Hit Points, Vaelroot is truly destroyed and the violet growth it spread begins, slowly, to wilt. Award the entire three-phase encounter's XP now.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Vaelroot casts one of the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +12 to hit with spell attacks}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Vaelroot makes two Paradox Lash attacks, or it casts a spell and makes one Paradox Lash attack.

action.name: Paradox Lash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 12}, reach 15 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Force damage (the forbidden color; see that trait) plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Radiant or Necrotic damage (Vaelroot's choice).

action.name: Reshape the Scene
entries: Vaelroot tears a feature of the battlefield into raw paradox. Choose one within 90 feet; where it stood, a seam of starry under-sky is left in the ground:

items.name: A stone or boulder.
items.entry: It detonates into the forbidden color: each creature within 15 feet makes a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw, taking 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Force damage on a failure, or half on a success.

items.name: A tree or large plant.
items.entry: It unmakes upward into a 20-foot-high pillar of grasping under-sky: each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 20} Strength saving throw or is pulled in and {@condition restrained} (escape {@dc 20}), taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Force damage; the space becomes {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3}.

items.name: Grass, undergrowth, or soil.
items.entry: A 15-foot square is erased to blank white under-sky: it becomes {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3}, and a creature that ends its turn there takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Psychic damage and must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn as it briefly forgets where—and that—it is.

items.name: No feature present?
items.entry: Vaelroot rips one out of the under-sky in an unoccupied space within 90 feet, then immediately uses one option above.

action.name: Unmaking Bloom {@recharge 5}
entries: A flower of pure paradox opens at a point within 150 feet and collapses. Each creature in a 40-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw, taking 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Force damage (the forbidden color) on a failure, or half on a success. The area is left as buckled under-sky {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} until the end of Vaelroot's next turn.

bonus.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot melds into the ground and steps out of any unoccupied space it can see within 90 feet through the under-sky, leaving a seam of stars where it appears.

reaction.name: Recoil into the Veins
entries: When Vaelroot takes damage, it can use Sapway Step, melding away from the blow before it lands fully (it still takes the damage). Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Vaelroot can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Lash
entries: Vaelroot makes one Paradox Lash attack.

legendary.name: Sapway Step
entries: Vaelroot uses Sapway Step.

legendary.name: Unmake (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Vaelroot uses Reshape the Scene, or Unmaking Bloom if it is available.

entries.name: Neither Light nor Shadow — Only the Wound
entries: In its last aspect, Vaelroot stops pretending to be a season at all. The druid burns away into the raw paradox at its core—the forbidden color, the under-sky, the blank white nothing of the Apagados—and the battlefield itself becomes its body. Stones detonate, trees unmake into pillars of stars, and the grass simply stops existing. It is not light and it is not shadow. It is the wound Vorthrax opened, blooming.

entries.name: Design: Three-Phase Boss for Six 15th-Level Heroes
entries: Vaelroot is one boss in three forms—Verdant Dawn (Plant, CR 15) → Withering Dusk (Fey, CR 16) → Unmade Bloom (Aberration, CR 17)—each rising at full Hit Points when the last falls. Tuned for {@b six} 15th-level characters equipped with a rare and a very rare item each: the full gauntlet sits around the {@b High} XP budget for that party, so expect a hard, climactic, but winnable fight of roughly 5–7 rounds. Legendary Resistance and legendary actions keep it relevant against six action economies, and the real engine is Reshape the Scene + Sapway Step: Vaelroot weaponizes whatever is on the map (a stone, a tree, the grass), and if you fight it on bare ground it just pulls terrain out of the under-sky. To make it easier, drop a phase or cut ~20% HP; to make it brutal, give it a lair full of stones and trees to reshape. Heavy battlefield control—keep moving, don't cluster, and watch the bleaching grass.

monster.name: Vorthrax

fly.condition: (he prefers to keep all four paws on the ground)

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
entries: If Vorthrax fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Wellspring of Mana
entries: Vorthrax's existence condenses mana out of nothing. Magic can't be suppressed or negated within 120 feet of him: antimagic effects (such as {@spell antimagic field|XPHB}) and dead-magic areas don't function there, and Vorthrax's attacks, spells, and wheels ignore them. He chose to sleep in a mana-starved waste so that his overflowing existence would drown no one.

trait.name: Force Breaker
entries: Vorthrax's claws shear bound energy apart. His Rend ignores Resistance and Immunity to Force damage, and effects of magical force (such as {@spell shield|XPHB}, {@spell wall of force|XPHB}, and {@spell forcecage|XPHB}) impede neither his attacks nor his movement: any such effect his claws strike, or that he moves through, is instantly destroyed.

trait.name: Paradox Husk
entries: While in Bright Light or in Darkness, Vorthrax has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. While in Dim Light—the twilight where his two halves meet—he loses this Resistance.

trait.name: The Three Wheels
entries: Three enormous Ioun stones orbit within 10 feet of Vorthrax, spinning ceaselessly. When a creature enters a space within 15 feet of Vorthrax or starts its turn there, one intact wheel of Vorthrax's choice scourges it: the creature makes a DC 24 saving throw (see below), taking 16 ({@damage 3d10}) damage of the wheel's type on a failed save. A creature can be scourged this way only once per turn.

items.name: Dawnwheel (Radiant; Strength save).
items.entry: While it orbits, it sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius.

items.name: Duskwheel (Necrotic; Constitution save).
items.entry: While it orbits, Vorthrax's Rend the Weave legendary action ends spells of level 9 and lower automatically.

items.name: Voidwheel (Force; Dexterity save).
items.entry: While it orbits, the teleport range of Vorthrax's Paradox Step doubles.

entries: Each wheel is a Large magic object: AC 22, HP 45, Immunity to Necrotic, Poison, Psychic, and Radiant damage. A destroyed wheel detonates: each creature within 10 feet of it makes a DC 24 Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 4d10}) damage of the wheel's type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Destroyed wheels reform only when Vorthrax returns from a rebirth that took 12 hours or longer. Full statblocks for the {@creature Dawnwheel|VorthraxParadox}, {@creature Duskwheel|VorthraxParadox}, and {@creature Voidwheel|VorthraxParadox}—including how each one moves—follow this creature.

trait.name: Paradox Rebirth
entries: Vorthrax cannot die by ordinary means. When he is reduced to 0 Hit Points, his body melts into a window of open night sky, time rewinds one second, and a new Vorthrax—same soul, same memories, new body—is dragged into the present, gaining one Paradox Mark.

The first time this happens during an encounter, he is gone only an instant: at the start of the next round he reappears with all his Hit Points in his space (or the nearest unoccupied space), and every condition and effect on him ends.

Each subsequent death sends him careening through Gaia's scorched xylem, lost between seconds. He is gone for 12 hours, and each death thereafter doubles the journey along this scale: 12 hours, 1 day, 1 week, 1 month, and so on—each crossing melts more of the World Tree's veins, and he loses his way in the dark between moments. Wherever he dies, the ground remains a permanent scar of open night sky (see the regional effects).
Nothing ends the cycle—not even salvation (see The Mended Soul). If Paradox Rebirth triggers during an encounter, award XP as if the party had defeated two CR 25 creatures.

trait.name: Paradox Mark
entries: Each Paradox Mark grants Vorthrax a cumulative, permanent +1 bonus to AC, attack rolls, and save DCs; 25 additional Hit Points (maximum and current); and an extra 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Force damage on his Rend. This statblock assumes no marks; a Vorthrax who has already died should arrive marked.

trait.name: Sapways of Gaia
entries: The planes hang from the World Tree, Gaia, and Vorthrax walks her veins. He can spend 1 minute melding into any wood or living plant connected to Gaia and emerge from any other branch, root, or leaf of the World Tree, on any plane. Every crossing scalds the xylem behind him: the passage withers, and a hair-thin seam of night sky is left in the wood.

trait.name: The Mended Soul
entries: Vorthrax is the mightiest creature of the Elemental Realm of Light fused with the mightiest spawn of the Primordial Darkness, and that fusion is the paradox that sustains him. If he is ever made whole—purified wholly into light, or surrendered wholly into shadow, by whatever rite, miracle, or {@spell wish|XPHB} can accomplish it—the paradox treats salvation as an execution: his past, unmended self steps out of the rewound second for a single purpose—to slay the mended Vorthrax, exactly as if he had been killed. The death plays out as in Paradox Rebirth: the mended body melts into a scar of open night sky, and the unmended self remains, one Paradox Mark stronger. There is no happy ending and no rest; saving Vorthrax is only another way of killing him, at the same price as all the others.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Vorthrax makes three Rend attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Rend
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 18}, reach 15 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 2d12 + 10}) Force damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Radiant or Necrotic damage (Vorthrax's choice).

action.name: Dark Star Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: Vorthrax exhales a collapsing star of fused light and darkness, which detonates at a point he can see within 150 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 25}, each creature in a 40-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Force damage. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage is disintegrated, as the {@spell disintegrate|XPHB} spell. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

The Sphere remains until the start of Vorthrax's next turn as a void of crushing gravity filled with magical Darkness that no vision—not even Truesight—can penetrate, and through which no sound can be created or pass (as the {@spell dark star|EGW} spell). A creature that enters the Sphere for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there repeats the saving throw.

bonus.name: Paradox Step
entries: Vorthrax slips one second sideways through time, teleporting up to 60 feet (120 feet while the Voidwheel orbits him) to an unoccupied space he can see, along with his wheels.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Vorthrax casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 23; +15 on spellcasting ability checks):

1e: {@spell plane shift|XPHB} (self only; along Gaia's branches)

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3 (4 in Lair). Immediately after another creature's turn, Vorthrax can expend a use to take one of the following actions. Vorthrax regains all expended uses at the start of each of his turns.

legendary.name: Rend the Weave
entries: Vorthrax casts {@spell dispel magic|XPHB} (level 5 version; +15 on the spellcasting ability check), targeting one creature, object, or magical effect he can perceive. While the Duskwheel orbits him, the casting instead ends spells of level 9 and lower automatically.

legendary.name: Paradox Lunge
entries: Vorthrax moves up to half his Speed or teleports up to 60 feet, then makes one Rend attack.

legendary.name: Wheelstorm (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The wheels howl and widen their orbits: each creature of Vorthrax's choice within 30 feet of him is scourged by an intact wheel of his choice, as in The Three Wheels trait.

entries.name: The Strongest Light, the Deepest Dark
entries: Vorthrax was firstborn of the Elemental Realm of Light: an archdraco—four wings, four paws—and the mightiest creature radiance ever shaped. Though his frame is built to rear up on two legs, he keeps all four paws pressed to the ground. He used to say the world deserved to carry his whole weight honestly; now no one is sure he remembers why. Light cannot burn him.

The Primordial Darkness, the mother-deep of the Elemental Realm of Shadow, poured itself into him to steal light's champion. It half succeeded: the graft took, and he is now as much shadow dragon as light, no rot or darkness able to touch him—but neither half would yield to the other. Light and dark fused, and the fusion drove him mad.

In a rare clear moment, Vorthrax dragged himself to a mana-starved waste far from every road and went to sleep, so that his lunacy would hurt no one. It was the kindest place he could choose: his mere existence condenses mana, and the starving land drinks the overflow. But the curse stirs whenever darkness draws near—when shadows reach for him, the madness flares, and he wakes.

entries.name: The Paradox
entries: Vorthrax cannot die. Whatever strikes him down, time flinches: the moment rewinds one second, and a new Vorthrax—new body, same memories—is hauled forward into the present. He does not do this consciously; it is a consequence of what he is, the strongest creature of light and the strongest creature of shadow occupying one impossible soul. Each death leaves him stronger and stranger.

The crossing is not free. The planes hang from the World Tree, Gaia, and each rebirth drags him through her xylem, melting the living veins as he passes. Where his body fell, the ground does not scar with stone or ash—it opens onto a night sky beneath the soil, a window of unfamiliar stars where the floor should be. And because the veins he travels are a little more ruined each time, he takes ever longer to find his way back: twelve hours, then a day, then a week, then a month.

There is no way out. If anyone ever truly saves him—returns him wholly to the light, or surrenders him wholly to the dark—the paradox answers the only way it knows how: his past, unmended self steps out of the rewound second and kills the mended one, as if salvation were merely another sword. The corpse opens its window of night sky, the unmended Vorthrax remains one mark stronger, and the cycle continues. There is no happy ending. There is no rest.

entries.name: The Corrupted Region
entries: The land around his sleeping-hollow turns slowly violet, and rifts of under-sky open in fields and cellar floors while the buildings above stand eerily firm. Folk who witness a rift open forget the faces of whoever stood beside them, and many wander off to re-live the last hour of their lives on a loop, talking to no one. The beasts that linger change in small wrong ways—hide re-tinted in impossible colors, eyes faintly starlit—and grow monstrously strong, like familiar creatures repainted for the end of the world.

entries.name: Running the Fight (Design Notes)
entries: Vorthrax is a solo, two-phase boss tuned for four 17th-level characters equipped with very rare and legendary magic items. Expect 5–7 rounds across both lives; if Paradox Rebirth triggers, award XP for two CR 25 monsters (150,000 XP total).

Dynamic levers: the three wheels are destructible sub-objectives (break the Voidwheel to halve his teleport, the Duskwheel to weaken his legendary {@spell dispel magic|XPHB}, the Dawnwheel to snuff his personal Bright Light); Paradox Husk rewards parties that engineer Dim Light around him; Force Breaker punishes {@spell wall of force|XPHB} and {@spell forcecage|XPHB} turtling; Dark Star Breath punishes clustering.

Tactics: he opens with Dark Star Breath on the tightest cluster, Paradox Steps onto the backline so the wheels scourge the casters, spends Rend the Weave on flight and the party's strongest buff, and saves Legendary Resistance for banishment and imprisonment effects. After his first death he returns at full Hit Points with a Paradox Mark—play the second life faster and crueler; he has already seen one second of the future.

The curse across a campaign: Death 1—returns at the start of the next round (full HP, +1 mark). Death 2—returns after 12 hours. Death 3—1 day. Death 4—1 week. Death 5—1 month, doubling thereafter. He comes back one mark stronger each time. The question is never how to kill Vorthrax; it is whether killing him is ever worth it.

monster.name: Dawnwheel
languages: understands the intent of Vorthrax but can't speak

trait.name: Bound Orbit (How It Moves)
entries: The Dawnwheel climbs. It rides the high arc of its orbit, wheeling over Vorthrax's shoulders like a sunrise that never finishes rising, its rim spinning so fast the gold blurs into one solid ring. It moves only with Vorthrax—sweeping overhead to hold its high station above him—and wherever he teleports it is already cresting the sky above the new spot. It never touches the ground.

trait.name: Paradox-Anchored
entries: The Dawnwheel acts on {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}'s initiative, takes no actions of its own, and orbits within 10 feet of him at all times, accompanying him whenever he moves or teleports. It can't be moved against its will or forced out of his orbit: it automatically fails checks made to relocate it and is immune to any effect that would move it without destroying it.

trait.name: Orbital Scourge (Radiant)
entries: Whenever a creature enters a space within 15 feet of the Dawnwheel for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature makes a {@dc 24} Strength saving throw, taking 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Radiant damage on a failed save. A creature can be scourged by the Dawnwheel only once per turn.

When the wheel is run alongside {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}, use his The Three Wheels trait instead, which lets only one wheel of his choice scourge a given creature each turn (rather than all three at once).

trait.name: Dawn's Vigil
entries: While the Dawnwheel orbits, it sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. That Bright Light denies Vorthrax's enemies the Dim Light they need to pierce his Paradox Husk near him—destroying the Dawnwheel is one way to drown his space in the twilight where he becomes vulnerable.

trait.name: Shatter
entries: When the Dawnwheel drops to 0 Hit Points, its bound light detonates: each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The wheel is destroyed and reforms intact only when Vorthrax returns from a rebirth that took 12 hours or longer (see his Paradox Rebirth).

One of three Ioun stones torn from Vorthrax's hoard and remade by the paradox, the Dawnwheel is his light-half given a body: a small captive sunrise that holds the high station of the orbit and burns away the gentle twilight that would otherwise show his foes where he can be hurt.
Run the Dawnwheel as part of the Vorthrax encounter; the wheels grant no XP of their own.

monster.name: Duskwheel
languages: understands the intent of Vorthrax but can't speak

trait.name: Bound Orbit (How It Moves)
entries: The Duskwheel sinks. It drags along the low arc of its orbit, grinding just above the soil like a dying sun that refuses to set—turning slow and heavy, trailing a grey smear of afterimage half a second behind itself. As it circles it leans toward the nearest living warmth, as though it means to put that warmth out. When Vorthrax moves, it wallows after him: last to arrive, and always somehow in the way.

trait.name: Paradox-Anchored
entries: The Duskwheel acts on {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}'s initiative, takes no actions of its own, and orbits within 10 feet of him at all times, accompanying him whenever he moves or teleports. It can't be moved against its will or forced out of his orbit: it automatically fails checks made to relocate it and is immune to any effect that would move it without destroying it.

trait.name: Orbital Scourge (Necrotic)
entries: Whenever a creature enters a space within 15 feet of the Duskwheel for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature makes a {@dc 24} Constitution saving throw, taking 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Necrotic damage on a failed save. A creature can be scourged by the Duskwheel only once per turn.

When the wheel is run alongside {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}, use his The Three Wheels trait instead, which lets only one wheel of his choice scourge a given creature each turn (rather than all three at once).

trait.name: Dusk's Unraveling
entries: While the Duskwheel orbits, Vorthrax's Rend the Weave legendary action ends spells of level 9 and lower automatically, requiring no ability check. If the Duskwheel is destroyed, Rend the Weave reverts to a normal level 5 {@spell dispel magic|XPHB}.

trait.name: Shatter
entries: When the Duskwheel drops to 0 Hit Points, its bound rot detonates: each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The wheel is destroyed and reforms intact only when Vorthrax returns from a rebirth that took 12 hours or longer (see his Paradox Rebirth).

The Duskwheel is Vorthrax's shadow-half made into a millstone—a sun that died mid-set, grinding along the bottom of the orbit. The necrotic cold pouring off it lets him tear spells out of the world with a careless swipe.
Run the Duskwheel as part of the Vorthrax encounter; the wheels grant no XP of their own.

monster.name: Voidwheel
languages: understands the intent of Vorthrax but can't speak

trait.name: Bound Orbit (How It Moves)
entries: The Voidwheel stutters. It does not orbit so much as skip—blinking from point to point along its path, each position a frame out of order, so that it seems to hang in three places at once before snapping into one. It is only ever half-present in this second. When Vorthrax teleports, the Voidwheel does not follow him: it was already there, waiting, a heartbeat before he arrives.

trait.name: Paradox-Anchored
entries: The Voidwheel acts on {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}'s initiative, takes no actions of its own, and orbits within 10 feet of him at all times, accompanying him whenever he moves or teleports. It can't be moved against its will or forced out of his orbit: it automatically fails checks made to relocate it and is immune to any effect that would move it without destroying it.

trait.name: Orbital Scourge (Force)
entries: Whenever a creature enters a space within 15 feet of the Voidwheel for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Force damage on a failed save. A creature can be scourged by the Voidwheel only once per turn.

When the wheel is run alongside {@creature Vorthrax|VorthraxParadox}, use his The Three Wheels trait instead, which lets only one wheel of his choice scourge a given creature each turn (rather than all three at once).

trait.name: Void's Skip
entries: While the Voidwheel orbits, the range of Vorthrax's Paradox Step bonus action increases from 60 feet to 120 feet. If the Voidwheel is destroyed, his Paradox Step range drops back to 60 feet. (Being force itself, the Voidwheel is not immune to Force damage—it is the easiest of the three to break for a party that can deal it.)

trait.name: Shatter
entries: When the Voidwheel drops to 0 Hit Points, its bound force detonates: each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 24} Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Force damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The wheel is destroyed and reforms intact only when Vorthrax returns from a rebirth that took 12 hours or longer (see his Paradox Rebirth).

The Voidwheel is the seam between Vorthrax's two halves—the cut itself, given mass. It never quite agrees on where it is, skipping a frame ahead of the present, and while it spins he borrows that same stutter to fold the distance his steps must cross.
Run the Voidwheel as part of the Vorthrax encounter; the wheels grant no XP of their own.

monster.name: Aureli, Treasurer-General of the Shogun

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Aureli fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Sunlit Heritage
entries: Aureli is a sun elf wreathed in daylight. She sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet, has Resistance to Radiant damage (already listed), and can't be {@condition blinded}.

trait.name: Floating Tanegashima
entries: Five spectral matchlock muskets orbit Aureli's back and fire at a thought. She makes Floating Musket attacks without needing a free hand, ignores their loading time, and can fire them in any direction.

trait.name: Inspiring War Banner
entries: Aureli's command banner is a Large object planted in her space or an adjacent space (AC 15, 40 Hit Points, {@b immune} to Poison and Psychic damage, {@b vulnerable} to Fire damage). While the banner stands, allied creatures within 30 feet of it—including those Aureli summons—add 1d6 to their attack rolls. If the banner is destroyed, this benefit ends; Aureli can plant a fresh banner with a Bonus Action (Plant the Banner).

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Aureli casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}; {@hitYourSpellAttack +10}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Aureli makes three Floating Musket attacks, or she casts a spell and makes one Floating Musket attack.

action.name: Floating Musket
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 60/220 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Sunfire Volley {@recharge 5}
entries: All five muskets fire as one, raking the field with sunfire in a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} 120 feet long and 30 feet wide, originating from Aureli. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}, each creature in that area. {@actSaveFail} 42 ({@damage 12d6}) Radiant damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. Aureli's own allies caught in the line are affected normally—she sweeps it where it pays best.

action.name: Conjure Treasury Statue
entries: Aureli raises a momentary statue of precious metal—a Large object—in an unoccupied space within 30 feet. It lasts 1 minute, until destroyed, or until she dismisses it (no action). She can have only one statue at a time; raising a new one ends the previous. Choose one:

items.name: Adamantine Bulwark.
items.entry: AC 20, 80 Hit Points, immovable. It fully blocks its space (Total Cover) and is immune to the extra damage of Critical Hits. A defensive wall—note it blocks Aureli's own line of sight too, so she places it with care.

items.name: Mithral Striker.
items.entry: AC 17, 30 Hit Points. At the start of each of Aureli's turns it can move up to 30 feet and make one slam: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft., 13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage.

items.name: Silver Idol.
items.entry: AC 13, 20 Hit Points. While it stands, a creature within 15 feet of it {@b can't regain Hit Points}—the baleful silver light unmakes every cure. It is a soft target on purpose (low AC, few Hit Points): shatter it, or step outside its 15-foot radius, to heal again. Aureli can raise the Silver Idol only once per encounter.

items.name: Gilded Effigy.
items.entry: AC 15, 30 Hit Points. When it is destroyed or its duration ends, it bursts into molten gold and light: each creature within 15 feet makes a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) damage—half Radiant, half Fire—on a failure, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Summon the Shadow Kensei (2/Day)
entries: Aureli sweeps her war fan and calls her guard out of the dark. One {@creature Solar Elf Shadow Kensei|VorthraxParadox} appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet that is in Dim Light or Darkness (or any unoccupied space within 30 feet if none qualify) and acts on Aureli's initiative, immediately after her turn. Aureli can't have more than three Kensei active at once.

bonus.name: Sunburst Pellet {@recharge 4}
entries: Aureli hurls a bomb of packed daylight at a point within 60 feet. Each creature within a 10-foot radius makes a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Radiant damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn; on a success, half damage and not Blinded.

bonus.name: Plant the Banner
entries: If her Inspiring War Banner is not currently standing, Aureli plants a fresh one in her space or an adjacent space.

bonus.name: Fey Step (3/Day)
entries: Aureli teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space she can see, vanishing in a flicker of daylight. (She still takes only one Bonus Action per turn, choosing among Fey Step, Sunburst Pellet, and Plant the Banner.)

reaction.name: Deflecting Fan
entries: When Aureli is hit by a ranged attack, she flicks her war fan and turns the hit into a miss (she can deflect arrows and bullets alike). She can use this reaction only once per round, so a creature making several ranged attacks loses only one of them to it.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 2. Immediately after another creature's turn, Aureli can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Snap Shot
entries: Aureli makes one Floating Musket attack. She can use Snap Shot no more than once per round, and not at all on a round she has used Sunfire Volley.

legendary.name: Field Command
entries: One allied creature within 30 feet that can see or hear Aureli uses its Reaction to either move up to its Speed or make one weapon attack.

legendary.name: Raise Statue (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: Aureli uses Conjure Treasury Statue.

entries.name: The Shogun's Golden Hand
entries: Aureli is a sun elf who keeps the Shogun's war-chest and the Shogun's war-plans in the same ledger—Treasurer-General and War-Counselor both. On the field she fights like an accountant balances a book: every shot, every conjured statue, every soldier called from the dark is an entry placed exactly where it pays best. She carries a command banner that steadies her troops and a feathered war fan that flicks arrows out of the air, and five spectral matchlock muskets drift at her back, firing wherever her attention falls. From her open hand she spends the treasury itself—momentary idols of adamantine, mithral, silver, and gold—as freely as another commander spends coin.

entries.name: Design: Elite Commander for 5 Level-11 PCs (3 Clerics, 2 Rangers)
entries: Aureli (CR 15) plus the {@creature Solar Elf Shadow Kensei|VorthraxParadox}s she summons (CR 6 each) are tuned to be a reliable {@b Hard} set-piece for five well-equipped 11th-level characters whose party is three Clerics and two Rangers (a Beast Master with a pet and a long-range archer). 2024 DMG budgets for 5x level 11: Low 14,500 / Moderate 25,000 / High 36,000 XP.

{@b Default presentation = Hard.} Open with Aureli + 2 Kensei (~17,600 fielded XP) and let her Summon (2/Day, one at a time, max 3 Kensei active) top the field back up to three. Keep fielded value around 17,600–19,900 for Hard. The XP math {@i undercounts} this fight because the banner (+1d6 to-hit on every ally), the summons (restored action economy), and the legendary muskets are force-multipliers the budget doesn't capture—so the levers below are written conservatively on purpose.

{@b The counters are deliberate taxes, all partial against this party.} Deflecting Fan stops only one ranged hit per round, so a Ranger with multiple attacks loses just one shot (and it no longer redirects at allies—no friendly fire). Sunburst Pellet (now on a recharge, not every round) Blinds only on a failed Con save and only for one turn—Lesser Restoration, spreading out, or closing to melee all answer it. Aureli {@i resists} Radiant rather than being immune, and the Kensei no longer resist Radiant at all, so the three Clerics keep full damage against the minions and half against the boss. The Silver Idol is the sharp exception: while it stands, healing within 15 feet is {@b shut off entirely}—the one hard answer to the three-Cleric engine. But it's a soft AC-13/20-HP object the party can shatter in a turn (or simply step out of), and she raises it only once per fight, so the lock is always breakable. Every other lever taxes without erasing a class's turn; with the Idol up, don't {@i also} dump Aureli's biggest burst on a PC trapped in its radius—or place it away from where the Clerics must stand—unless you mean to run the Deadly variant.

{@b The banner and statues are the puzzle.} Destroying the Inspiring War Banner (40 HP, vulnerable to Fire) strips +1d6 to-hit from every Kensei at once—high-value for the archer. The Adamantine Bulwark blocks line of sight and fire (for Aureli too); the Gilded Effigy is a delayed bomb (move away before it pops); the Mithral Striker chases the backline; the Silver Idol pins healing once. She holds only one statue at a time, choosing what hurts the party most that round.

{@b Escalation to Deadly (only if the 3 Clerics are visibly out-sustaining her):} field a 3rd–4th Kensei, keep the Silver Idol standing, raise Legendary Action Uses to 3, and have the Kensei Shadow-Step onto the archers every round. {@b Ease it} by skipping the Silver Idol, summoning once, and going light on Darkness so the Kensei can't teleport freely (their Shadow Step needs Dim Light or Darkness—a party that lights the field shuts it off).

trait.name: Shadow Step
entries: While in Dim Light or Darkness, the kensei can take a Bonus Action to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see that is also in Dim Light or Darkness. It then has Advantage on the first melee attack it makes before the end of this turn.

trait.name: Solar Resilience
entries: The kensei has Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition blinded} (Aureli's light bombs don't blind her own guard).

trait.name: Steady Aim
entries: If the kensei doesn't move during its turn, it has Advantage on its Tanegashima Musket attack rolls and those shots deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Martial Arts
entries: The kensei uses Dexterity for its unarmed strikes and kensei weapons, and can make one unarmed strike as a Bonus Action (see Flurry of Blows).

spellcasting.name: Shadow Arts
headerEntries: The kensei channels shadow ki to cast the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability. It can see normally through any magical Darkness it creates:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kensei makes two attacks, using Moonlit Katana or Tanegashima Musket in any combination.

action.name: Moonlit Katana
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Necrotic damage from its shadow-honed edge.

action.name: Tanegashima Musket
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 60/220 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: The kensei makes two unarmed strikes: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft., 7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage each.

bonus.name: Vanish
entries: The kensei takes the {@action Hide} action—trivial to pull off inside its own {@spell darkness|XPHB} or any Dim Light. (It takes only one Bonus Action per turn: Flurry of Blows, Shadow Step, or Vanish.)

reaction.name: Deflect Shot
entries: When the kensei is hit by a ranged attack, it reduces the damage by 12 ({@damage 1d10 + 7}).

entries.name: Sun-Blooded, Shadow-Trained
entries: Aureli's personal guard: sun elves who took the Way of Shadow, fighting from the dark with shadow-edged katana, bursts of unarmed strikes, and matchlock muskets identical to the merchants' issue. They step from shadow to shadow across the battlefield, appearing at a flank, firing, and vanishing before a bowstring can answer—and the sunlight in their blood keeps Aureli's own blinding light from touching them.

monster.name: Sawjaw Rex, the Steam Tyrant

from: scaled hide and bolted iron plates

languages: understands the shouted commands of its handler but can't speak

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If Sawjaw Rex fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead. (Its Runaway Boiler form receives its own, separate uses.)

trait.name: Scalding Exhaust
entries: The boiler on its back vents constantly. A creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of Sawjaw Rex, or that hits it with a melee attack from within 5 feet, takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage.

trait.name: Reignition
entries: Sawjaw Rex is a living beast piloting—and piloted by—the machine bolted to it. The first time it is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it doesn't die: the boiler roars, clamps onto the failing body, and seizes control of the corpse. At the start of what would have been its next turn (on the same initiative count), it rises as {@creature Sawjaw Rex, the Runaway Boiler|VorthraxParadox} in the same space, at that form's full Hit Points, with all conditions on it ended. Run both forms as one boss; award the encounter's XP only when the construct form is destroyed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Sawjaw Rex makes one Chainsaw Arm attack and one Bite attack. It can replace the Bite with one Steam Cannon attack.

action.name: Chainsaw Arm
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Slashing damage, and the grinding teeth open a Bleeding Wound: at the start of each of the target's turns it loses 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Hit Points until it receives any magical healing or a creature uses an action to make a {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check to stanch it. Multiple Bleeding Wounds on the same target don't stack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Steam Cannon
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 8}, range 80/240 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d8 + 1}) Fire damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked {@condition prone} by the blast of scalding air.

action.name: Spit Fire {@recharge 5}
entries: Sawjaw Rex belches a gout of fire in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Fire damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Sawjaw Rex can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Cannon Blast
entries: Sawjaw Rex makes one Steam Cannon attack. It can't use Cannon Blast more than twice per round.

legendary.name: Vent Steam
entries: Pressurized steam screams out. Each creature within 10 feet of Sawjaw Rex makes a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

legendary.name: Tail Sweep (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Sawjaw Rex sweeps its tail in a wide arc. Each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage and being knocked {@condition prone} on a failed save, or taking half damage on a success.

entries.name: The Steam-Smith's War-Beast
entries: A captured young tyrannosaur, rebuilt by a steam-smith into a walking siege engine: the right forelimb sawn off and replaced with a roaring chainsaw, a great pressure-boiler bolted to its back as both fuel-pack and cannon, and the throat lined to let it spit kindled fire. In life it is a maddened, augmented predator that charges anything that moves. But the boiler is more than a weapon—it is a dead-man's rig. When the beast finally falls, the machine does not. It was commissioned—and is guarded—by a company of merchant Musketeers who treat it as their most valuable, most dangerous piece of stock.

monster.name: Sawjaw Rex, the Runaway Boiler

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the construct fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh, separate from the Steam Tyrant's.)

trait.name: Overpressured Boiler
entries: The boiler riding the corpse's back is a separate Large object in the construct's space: AC 15, 40 Hit Points, {@b vulnerable} to Cold damage. The boiler does not share the construct's damage resistances—attacks against it use only its own defenses. A creature can choose to attack the boiler instead of the construct (it is an exposed target on the construct's back, in plain reach of melee and ranged attackers alike). While the boiler has Hit Points, the construct is in {@b overdrive}: its weapon attacks deal an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage (included below) and it has 4 Legendary Action Uses instead of 3. If the boiler is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the construct enters {@b meltdown}: it loses the overdrive bonuses immediately, and at the start of its next turn it uses Boiler Detonation and is destroyed (the boiler whines and gushes steam in the meantime—a clear, full-round warning to clear the blast).

trait.name: Scalding Cloud
entries: At the start of each of the construct's turns, steam floods the space around it. Each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. This area is lightly obscured to creatures that rely on sight.

trait.name: Cold-Shock Vulnerability
entries: The boiler runs dangerously hot. Whenever the construct or its boiler takes Cold damage, the construct can't take {@b Reactions} or {@b Legendary Actions} until the end of its next turn as its overheated plates seize and crack.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The construct makes one Chainsaw Arm attack and one Ram attack.

action.name: Chainsaw Arm
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage (from overdrive; the Fire damage is lost while the boiler is destroyed/in meltdown).

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage (from overdrive), and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Overloaded Cannon {@recharge 5}
entries: The boiler-cannon over-fires a roaring shell of superheated air at a point the construct can see within 120 feet. Each creature in a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The area becomes {@quickref Difficult Terrain||3} of smoldering slag until the end of the construct's next turn.

action.name: Boiler Detonation
entries: {@b Triggered—not an action the construct chooses.} {@b If the construct drops to 0 Hit Points}, the boiler ruptures immediately as the wreck collapses. {@b If instead the boiler is destroyed} (see Overpressured Boiler), the construct doesn't explode at once—it enters meltdown and the boiler ruptures at the start of its next turn, giving everyone a full round to scatter. When it ruptures, each creature within 20 feet makes a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The construct is then destroyed, collapsing into inert, burning scrap.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3 (4 while the boiler is intact). Immediately after another creature's turn, the construct can expend a use to take one of the following actions. It regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns. (It can't take Legendary Actions on a turn after it has taken Cold damage; see Cold-Shock Vulnerability.)

legendary.name: Saw
entries: The construct makes one Chainsaw Arm attack. It can't use Saw more than twice per round.

legendary.name: Vent Burst
entries: A jet of steam erupts in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave dex} {@dc 16}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Fire damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

legendary.name: Lurch (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The construct moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks and can make one Ram attack at any point during the move.

entries.name: The Machine Outlives the Beast
entries: The tyrant is dead; the engine is not. Pistons drive the slack limbs, the chainsaw still screams, and the over-stoked boiler floods the wreck with scalding steam as it lurches forward on borrowed legs. It no longer hunts—it just keeps going, hotter and hotter, until something cracks the boiler or it simply blows itself apart.

entries.name: Design: Two-Stage Solo Boss — Hard Encounter for 4 Level-8 PCs
entries: Built for a well-equipped, optimally built party of four 8th-level characters (Paladin tank, Life Cleric, Hunter/Ranger, Sorcerer). One boss in two stages: {@creature Sawjaw Rex, the Steam Tyrant|VorthraxParadox} (Monstrosity, CR 11) → at 0 HP it rises as the Runaway Boiler (Construct, CR 12) at full Hit Points. The two stages total about 15,600 XP, between the {@b Moderate} and {@b High} budgets for that party in the 2024 DMG—i.e. a genuine {@b Hard} fight that a strong party should win at a cost. CRs are kept moderate on purpose so the attack bonuses (+10), save DCs (15–16), and per-hit damage stay {@b fair} for level 8; the difficulty comes from the second life, high Hit Points, and Legendary Actions, not from unfair numbers.

{@b Anti-rocket-tag guards (built in).} Repeatable legendary attacks are capped at twice per round (Cannon Blast in Stage 1, Saw in Stage 2) so the boss can't dump its whole legendary pool onto one PC and one-shot a caster. Stage 2 is fire-{@i resistant} (not immune), so a fire Sorcerer still contributes—but Cold is the boss's weakness and the smart pivot.

The {@b boiler} is the puzzle and a genuine shortcut: 40 HP, vulnerable to Cold, and it does {@i not} share the construct's b/p/s resistance, so martials and the Ranger can chip it directly. Cracking it skips the construct's remaining Hit Points, but it then melts down and detonates at the start of the boss's {@i next} turn (28 fire, 20 ft)—a telegraphed 'crack it, then scatter' play rather than a punish on whoever lands the blow. The Sorcerer's Cold spells both exploit the vulnerability and shut off the boss's Reactions and Legendary Actions for a round (Cold-Shock). The Life Cleric should pre-heal before Spit Fire / Overloaded Cannon and answer the Chainsaw's Bleeding Wound (any magical healing ends it); the Paladin's Aura of Protection is the linchpin against the many Dex/Con saves—keep the party inside it.

{@b Adding the Musketeers.} Sawjaw Rex alone is already Hard, so don't simply pile minions on top. Pick one: (1) run the {@creature Merchant Musketeer|VorthraxParadox}s and {@creature Merchant Bombardier|VorthraxParadox}s as a {@b warm-up wave} before the beast is loosed; (2) add only {@b 2–3} of them alongside the boss and drop the boss's Legendary Action Uses by 1 (or skip the Runaway Boiler stage); or (3) have them arrive as {@b mid-fight reinforcements} once the boss hits its second stage. The {@creature Merchant Captain|VorthraxParadox} is an elite—treat one Captain as roughly a whole extra encounter slot.

{@b DM dials.} Too hard: drop Legendary Action Uses to 2, cut the boiler to 25 HP, or reduce Spit Fire / Overloaded Cannon to 6d6. Too easy (e.g. the party hard-locks the mindless construct after its Legendary Resistances are gone): give the construct a 3rd Legendary Resistance, raise the boiler to 55 HP, or restore Stage 2 to 228 HP. The detonation is telegraphed on purpose—reward players who reposition.

trait.name: Steady Aim
entries: If the musketeer doesn't move during its turn, it has Advantage on its Musket attack rolls and those shots deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Musket
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, range 60/220 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Piercing damage from a loud black-powder shot (an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) if the musketeer held position this turn; see Steady Aim).

action.name: Bayonet Rapier
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Smoke Pellet (1/Day)
entries: The musketeer drops a smoke pellet, lightly obscuring a 5-foot-radius area around itself until the start of its next turn, and can then move up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

entries.name: Merchants with Muskets
entries: The hired guns of a wealthy trade company—usually human, always well-paid—who escort caravans and, lately, a steam-driven war-beast. They fight from range in disciplined lines, planting their feet to steady the shot, and slip away behind smoke when the line breaks.

trait.name: Volatile Satchel
entries: When the bombardier drops to 0 Hit Points, its satchel of bombs goes off. Each creature within 10 feet makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Bomb
entries: The bombardier hurls a bomb at a point it can see within 60 feet. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

action.name: Hatchet
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Smoke Bomb {@recharge 5}
entries: The bombardier throws a smoke bomb at a point within 60 feet. A 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of thick smoke spreads there, heavily obscuring the area until the start of the bombardier's next turn.

entries.name: Alchemy for Sale
entries: Where the line of muskets can't reach, the company sends a bombardier—part chemist, part smuggler—lobbing alchemical bombs over cover and laying smoke to break line of sight. Killing one is risky: the satchel still has a few live bombs in it.

trait.name: Steady Aim
entries: If the captain doesn't move during its turn, it has Advantage on its Fine Musket attack rolls and those shots deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Well-Paid Nerves
entries: The captain and its allies within 30 feet of it have Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The captain makes two attacks, using Fine Musket or Saber in any combination.

action.name: Fine Musket
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 6}, range 60/220 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Piercing damage (an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) with Steady Aim).

action.name: Saber
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Grapeshot Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: The captain fires a blunderbuss in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSave dex} {@dc 14}, each creature there. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Piercing damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Fire by Ranks (1/Turn)
entries: The captain barks a firing order. Up to three allied {@creature Merchant Musketeer|VorthraxParadox}s or {@creature Merchant Bombardier|VorthraxParadox}s within 30 feet that can see or hear the captain can each use their Reaction to immediately make one ranged attack (a Musket shot or a thrown Bomb). Each ally can be commanded by Fire by Ranks only once per round.

entries.name: The Quartermaster
entries: Every merchant company has one: the quartermaster who signs the contracts, prices the cargo, and—when negotiations fail—commands the firing line. The captain rarely needs to shoot much personally; one shouted order turns the whole company into a single volley.

{@b Encounter use:} a Captain is an elite, not a mook—count it as roughly a full extra encounter slot. With Fire by Ranks it sharply raises the threat of a group of Musketeers/Bombardiers, so add it only when you want the merchant company to be the {@i main} fight, or pair one Captain with 2–4 mooks rather than fielding it alongside Sawjaw Rex at full strength.

legendaryGroup.name: Vorthrax
regionalEffects: The region around Vorthrax's sleeping-hollow (typically a 6-mile radius, centered where Gaia's bark runs thin) is warped by his paradox, creating the following effects:

items.name: Violet Verdance.
items.entry: Stone, water, and living things slowly shift toward violet and ultraviolet hues. Buildings stand eerily firm and pristine, even where the ground beneath them has opened.

items.name: The Under-Sky.
items.entry: Rifts in the earth within 1 mile reveal not soil but a vault of night sky dense with unfamiliar stars. A creature that falls into a rift plunges "down" into that sky for {@dice 1d4} rounds before reality reasserts itself and deposits the creature, {@condition prone|XPHB}, at the rift's lip.

items.name: Hour-Echoes.
items.entry: When a new rift opens, each Beast and Humanoid within 300 feet of it must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw or forget everyone who was near it when the rift appeared and become compelled to re-live the last hour of its life, repeating its movements and conversations with no one. Each repetition frays the mind a little further, at the DM's discretion.

items.name: Paradox-Touched.
items.entry: Creatures that lair within 1 mile for more than a month are slowly re-colored in impossible tints—violet hide, starlit eyes—and grow unnaturally mighty, like familiar beasts refit for the end of the world. The DM can represent this by doubling such a creature's Hit Points and granting it a +2 bonus to AC, attack rolls, and save DCs.

items.name: Wellspring.
items.entry: Mana pools where there was none: dead-magic areas in the region rekindle, candle flames burn violet at dusk, and minor wonders—drifting motes of light, shadows that whisper—occur unbidden.

regionalEffects: If Vorthrax dies, these effects do not fade. Where he died, the land never heals: the spot remains a permanent window onto the under-sky, and Gaia's xylem withers for a mile around it, closing that branch-road of the World Tree forever.
